description_long: This object is a large, arched stone block with a rough, weathered surface and visible chisel marks. The stone is thick and solid, with a slightly irregular curvature and chipped edges, suggesting it has endured significant wear over time. Its coloration is predominantly gray, with subtle variations and darker patches, likely from exposure to the elements. The underside of the arch is less textured, showing some smoother areas, while the outer surface displays a grid-like pattern of shallow grooves. The block appears to be a fragment of a larger architectural or infrastructural element, such as a segment of a stone arch or barrier. Its substantial mass and robust construction indicate it was designed to serve as a durable obstacle or boundary in an urban or historical setting.
description: A large, arched gray stone block with a rough, weathered surface, chipped edges, and visible chisel marks, likely from an architectural or infrastructural context.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full arch of the stone block, highlighting its curvature, rough surface, and chipped lower edges.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes the thickness of the block and the irregular, jagged edge on one side.
description_view_2: The underside view reveals the hollow arch, with smoother interior surfaces and some darker discoloration.
description_view_3: The opposite side view displays the block's height and the grid-like pattern of grooves, as well as additional chipping along the edge.
attribute_location_context: fragment of a historical urban barrier or old city wall
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set into a masonry arch
attribute_weathering_pattern: Chipped corners and edges, with surface roughness and some discoloration indicating prolonged exposure to the elements.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: forms part of a continuous arch or barrier
